query: Your Chat UI Just Got an AI Roommate complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Bug Report: Severe Interface Issues in Chat - Google AI Studio - Google AI Developers Forum
results.content:
# Bug Report: Severe Interface Issues in Chat

Hello!  
Today I encountered several serious interface problems in the chat:

Messages cannot be selected or highlighted.

When scrolling, the interface jumps erratically — the screen “snaps” to random positions instead of moving smoothly.

It’s impossible to edit messages properly — the text cursor doesn’t appear or doesn’t move to the desired position.

In edit mode, the chat does not scroll at all, making it impossible to view or edit longer messages.

These issues make it extremely difficult to use the chat interface — editing and navigation are almost completely broken.  
The problems appeared today and persist after refreshing the page and clearing cache.

I’m seeing this too! In Chrome, Safari on Windows, Mac, and iOS.

Hi @Balinor, [...] ### Related topics

| Collection of interface problems  Google AI Studio ai-studio ,  ui | 13 | 470 | January 2, 2026 |
| Chat in Google AI Studio cannot select text! (temporary fix)  Google AI Studio ai-studio ,  ui | 3 | 181 | October 28, 2025 |
| [Subject: [Bug Report] Flawed Virtual Scrolling Implementation in <ms-prompt-scrollbar> Causes Uncontrollable Jumping](  Google AI Studio ai-studio ,  bug | 1 | 121 | December 12, 2025 |

Powered by Discourse, best viewed with JavaScript enabled [...] Hi @Balinor,

Welcome to the Forum,

The issue with message/text selection has been resolved and I haven’t encountered any problems in edit mode when editing long messages.

Additionally, a fix for the scrolling issue has been rolled out to all users.

Thank you for your patience.

Yeah, I’m having the same issue too.  
Did you manage to find any fix?  
Looks like more and more people are starting to run into this problem.

Should be fixed now. I don’t think i’m encountering these issues anymore.

The developers have already fixed this problem - at least I don’t encounter it anymore.

results.title: When I post about AI chat app problems, I get loads of "chat is the future of all UI" replies. When I post about AI chat app benefits, I get loads of "chat is a terrible UI" replies. So I… | Luke Wroblewski | 34 comments
results.content:
1mo 

 Report this comment

Chat as a UI only works if you know what you want already. Sometimes exploration is the goal and it’s a lot more likely someone will just click the back button vs if they don’t have a very specific goal in mind.

Like Reply  1 Reaction

Øyvind Rage 🚀   1mo 

 Report this comment [...] Chat is the most general-purpose interface, but that’s exactly why it isn’t the only one we need. Chat is great for quickly capturing user intent without forcing anyone to “learn” a new UI. But as soon as you need more precision, control over steps, or opportunities to explore, you want to shift into more opinionated interfaces. It's important that context stays visible and doesn’t disappear in a conversation thread, and that affordances are clear so people actually know what’s possible instead of having to guess. For example, in data-driven work, you might draft what you need in chat but then refine, filter, and visualize it instead of writing more text. With creative tools, you can start by describing your intent in words, then adjust layers or parameters directly within the canvas. And [...] 1mo 

 Report this comment

It's a journey and where we currently are, I don't fall on either side of the binary. Voice, chat, text will all remain components of any contextual interaction with computers in the future. So I concur with your point: "We’re very early in the evolution of AI-powered software and lots of different and useful interfaces for interacting with AI will emerge."

Like Reply  3 Reactions   4 Reactions

# Why My Frustrating Fight with an AI Chatbot Made Me Rethink Technology

The AI Almanac

The AI Almanac

3 min read

·

Dec 10, 2024

--

Today I had a strange fight with AI chatbot, and it is very frustrating.

If you are not a member. Please click here.

In a world where AI chatbots have become our virtual assistants, companions, and problem-solvers, you’d think they’d make life easier. But sometimes, even the most advanced technology can leave us frustrated and confused. [...] Today, I had a peculiar and exhausting encounter with an AI chatbot, one that left me questioning the “intelligence” in Artificial Intelligence.

I recently started drop shipping business in Etsy. I had a question about payments.

The conversation started innocently enough. Chatbot introduced itself as Kendra. So, Obviously, I thought it is a person.

I had a question, a specific query that I hoped the customer care would answer concisely. Instead, it launched into a verbose explanation, paragraph after paragraph of text that not only overcomplicated the issue but also repeated itself and keep posting same paragraphs.

Initially, I thought maybe I am not clear, it is a misunderstanding and tried to clarify my query. I got really frustrated with the same paragraphs. [...] Artificial Intelligence in Plain English

results.title: The Ai push is so frustrating especially in customer support, (also rant)
results.content:
Anyone else? Trying to cancel a subscription, an already daunting task for me (Ik ik but it be like that sometimes), and I’m hit with another Ai support chatbot. I leave the chat to find another way like an email and the chat disappears…forever…even when I try to redo the process. It’s small, but got damn this feel incredibly ableist (only internet support, lack of human nuance means easy to misunderstand, frustrating/overstimulating event etc.); if I can’t talk to someone about canceling this I’m gonna have to shell out like $300-400 and even if they do give me a refund I have to: Ask even though I’ve tried unsubscribing Have that money in my account and the money I need for bills Wait on a refund because they took my money even though I tried telling them not to. I’m just tired of it; [...] # The Ai push is so frustrating especially in customer support, (also rant)

Hey Open AI, when do plan on addressing and fixing this? Because you’ve ruined everything I spent months and months working on. All promises of tagging, indexing and filing away were lies. The only thing you did was break everything and tell the Chat bots to lie to us, your paid subscribers. It’s unacceptable. I want a refund for the last two months and I demand an ETA on when you plan on solving this problem or at the very least, offer a roll back solution so our life’s work isn’t flushed down the toilet.

 Tell me what to export right now and in what format. [...] What to know:

1.   This forum is community-run and maintained. Not all posts are monitored. To get in touch with OpenAI, reach out at 
2.   Discussions related to the ChatGPT app and plans will be hidden, and your account may be suspended. (We must keep conversations focused on technical topics related to building with OpenAI, for the sake of the larger community here.)
3.   Before posting a new topic, please search the forum for similar topics. This prevents duplicate discussions.
4.   Your use of this forum means you agree to the community guidelines, which include being nice, productive, and constructive. Read the full community guidelines: FAQ - OpenAI Developer Community

# Catastrophic Failures of ChatGpt that’s creating major problems for users

ChatGPTBugs [...] OpenAI needs to publish a real Data and Memory Use Policy, not just privacy fluff.

 And they need to guarantee:

   Data portability
   Data permanence
   Manual recovery options

  

## []( 21: :speech_balloon: 6. COMMUNICATE LIKE WE’RE ADULTS

Enough of the corporate therapy-speak. The gaslighty “we’re working to make memory better” while people lose years of work?

No.

Instead:

   Use specific, clear, dated language
   Tell us what broke and why
   Tell us if it’s gone for good
   Tell us what to expect

Trust is only possible with truth.

  

## []( 22: :repeat_button: 7. OPEN THE DOOR TO USER ADVOCACY
